name: Amazu Shuga (Pickled Pink Ginger)
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a sharp knife or a Beriner cutter, shave ginger root into paper-thin slices.
2. In a small, airtight container, combine vinegar, sugar, and salt.
3. Blanch the cooled ginger in boiling water for 30 seconds; then drain and cool the ginger.
4. Add the cooled ginger to the vinegar mixture and mix well.
5. Store the marinating ginger in the refrigerator.
6. Pickled ginger can be eaten after 24 hours of marinating and will keep several weeks.
7. Enjoy with sushi or noodles, or add the marinade to salads or sauces.
